title: carlina oligocephala subsp. oligocephala
intro: Carlina oligocephala subsp. oligocephala, also known as Carline Thistle, is a plant species belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties that have been used in traditional medicine to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, fever, and respiratory infections. The plant's roots and leaves contain sesquiterpene lactones, which are responsible for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, providing health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and boosting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Carline Thistle include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, making it a popular herb in traditional medicine.
